item.title: Jet fuel hits four-year high as shortages disrupt Middle East evacuation flights
item.description: Israel hits Iran and Lebanon on sixth day of conflict

item.title: Canadian Natural beats profit estimates on higher production
item.description: Fourth-quarter output jumped 12.8% from a year earlier

item.title: Business Brief: Untangling Roots
item.description: Why the retailer launched a ‘strategic review’

item.title: The economic question we forget to ask about AI
item.description: Forget the singularity and robot takeovers; what matters is who captures the value added

item.title: London’s investment halo is dimmed by war
item.description: The mining, energy and defence companies that powered the FTSE 100’s revival face uncertainty

item.title: CVCA urges Ottawa to put $750M fund behind growth-stage firms
item.description: The postCVCA urges Ottawa to put $750M fund behind growth-stage firmsappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Tanker attack in the northern Persian Gulf boosts oil and gas prices, signals Iran war is widening
item.description: Marks the first time a ship was hit beyond the Strait of Hormuz area

item.title: To understand why countries grow, look at their firms
item.description: The third way in development economics

item.title: India’s economy is not as big as economists thought
item.description: But it is growing faster

item.title: Americans’ electricity bills are up. Don’t blame AI
item.description: Were it not for data centres, prices might be even higher

item.title: U.S. stock futures slip as oil prices climb higher and Iran launches new attacks
item.description: Investors remain concerned about escalation of three-day-old conflict

item.title: Carbon removal coalition forms with goal of attracting $100-million in project investments
item.description: Leaders in Canada’s nascent industry joined with corporate, financial backers as well as federal government

item.title: Europe confronts threat of another energy crisis
item.description: Rerouting of LNG tanker bound for France to Asia signals escalating competition for gas supplies due to Iran war

item.title: Analysts’ views: rate markets are buying the weeks not months narrative on the Iran war
item.description: Despite the risks to inflation, forward rates remain optimistically priced

item.title: The Business of Formula 1
item.description: In this edition, 2026 rules revolution is set to be the biggest reset in modern F1; Audi’s measured takeover meets Cadillac’s splashy debut, and Apple’s Eddy Cue says why this is just the beginning’ for US Formula 1 coverage

item.title: Europe’s Leading Start-up Hubs 2026
item.description: Businesses specialising in complex technologies are emerging from the 180 centres highlighted here. Plus: how Munich became a leader in defence; climate tech proves strategic value; Portugal attracts start-ups seeking scale; Sweden’s AI success

item.title: The geopolitics of the global oceans treaty
item.description: It has been hailed as a diplomatic triumph, but some worry it could become a vehicle to increase China’s influence over the high seas

item.title: Climate tech proves strategic value in Europe’s push for autonomy
item.description: Start-ups producing green technologies play vital role in political shift towards security and independence

item.title: Iran launches missiles at Israel, U.S. bases as new Israeli strikes hit Lebanon
item.description: Fighting continues after American submarine sank an Iranian warship and Tehran threatened military and economic infrastructure across region

item.title: AI Minister tells OpenAI Canadian experts must assess flagged ChatGPT conversations
item.description: Chief executive Sam Altman agreed to the change, Evan Solomon says

item.title: Linamar reports $110.7-million in quarterly profit, up from previous year loss
item.description: Earnings amounted to $1.85 per diluted share, up from a net loss per diluted share of $3.78, company says

item.title: ‘War risk’ premium hitting businesses, consumers as Middle East conflict affects shipping
item.description: Knock-on effects could trickle down to consumers purchasing imported food, tech and clothes

item.title: What will change in Canada when generic Ozempic hits the market, according to our reporters
item.description: Readers asked about GLP-1 side effects, weight loss while on Ozempic and insurance coverage

item.title: How the closing of the Strait of Hormuz is affecting global oil markets
item.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard says it has taken control of the passageway, choking off the flow of Middle Eastern oil and gas

item.title: European pensions are a $30trn missed opportunity
item.description: If only more countries went Dutch

item.title: Companies entitled to Trump tariff refund after Supreme Court decision, judge rules
item.description: Trump administration could ultimately be on the hook for refunds worth $175-billion

item.title: Fed report says U.S. economy solid but notes disruption from Minnesota immigration crackdown
item.description: U.S. economic activity rose, prices increased and employment levels stable in recent weeks, Beige Book report says

item.title: Google ends legal battle with Epic Games, offers to lower app store commissions
item.description: Backed into a legal corner, Google is now prepared to decrease its baseline commissions

item.title: Elon Musk testifies in Twitter shareholder trial
item.description: Tesla CEO accused of making false and misleading statements that drove down Twitter’s stock price before he bought the company

item.title: Canada’s savings rates hold steady as global tensions threaten energy markets
item.description: This week’s highest savings account and guaranteed investment certificate rates

item.title: What the heirs to General Electric did next
item.description: Their success illustrates the benefits of breaking up—and more

item.title: Half of Cuba, including Havana, without power in widespread blackout
item.description: Island nation’s oil reserves continue to dwindle after Trump administration blocked shipments from Venezuela

item.title: Ottawa agrees to cover UN fees for Bay du Nord oil project that could reach $1-billion
item.description: Project would be built outside Canada’s exclusive economic zone, making it subject to international treaty

item.title: Rise of non-bank actors in debt markets could pose financial risks, Macklem says
item.description: Bank of Canada Governor says growing role of hedge funds, private credit in lending hasn’t been stress tested by periods of economic upheaval

item.title: U.S. private payrolls see largest increase in seven months in February, ADP data show
item.description: Private employment rose by 63,000 jobs last month, while January payrolls were revised sharply lower

item.title: When Canada and Australia team up, nobody can stop us
item.description: The two middle powers could have a powerful combined influence when working together, Scott Stirrett writes

item.title: Canada’s services economy declines further in February amid trade uncertainty
item.description: Activity in the sector has contracted for the last four months, S&P Global’s PMI data show

item.title: Travel remains disrupted by Iran war as governments scramble to bring citizens home
item.description: With airspace closed or heavily restricted across much of the Gulf, passengers have been stranded not only in the region but also in cities far from the fighting

item.title: Markets Wednesday: Indexes rise as oil steadies. Plus, Rosenberg’s top stock pick and TSX equities with the biggest analyst price target hikes
item.description: Canadian economist becomes ‘more bullish now than in many years’

item.title: Pro-Iran ‘hacktivists’ could target Canadian organizations, experts warn
item.description: Political activists, journalists and human rights advocates also likely to be targeted, the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security says

item.title: AGT Food and Ingredients stock stumbles on first day of trading
item.description: Agrifood giant’s shares shed 7.6 per cent value as company raises $450-million in IPO

item.title: Submarine bidders open to Ottawa splitting contract, say procurement speed setting new standard
item.description: Ottawa’s economic approach to contract could influence similar deals in the future, CEO of German submarine maker TKMS says

item.title: Telus planning to offer direct-to-cell satellite services in late 2026
item.description: Telecom takes equity stake in Texan satellite manufacturer AST SpaceMobile as part of commercial agreement

item.title: Iconic retailer Roots could be for sale as it launches strategic review
item.description: Toronto-based company has roughly 100 stores in Canada, two locations in the U.S. and another 100 partner-operated stores in Asia

item.title: Fertility rates are dropping, but not among older women – and that creates a ripple effect
item.description: Today, the birth rate for 37-year-old women is near triple what it was in 1977

item.title: Why war isn’t always good for defence stocks
item.description: They win only if governments want just enough weapons—but not too many

item.title: The nightmare war scenario is becoming reality in energy markets
item.description: The longer the war in the Gulf, the harsher the global economic fallout

item.title: The Iran war is a jolt to Dubai’s business model
item.description: Can the emirate keep people and investors coming?

item.title: The push to regulate chatbots after Tumbler Ridge is raising privacy fears
item.description: The postThe push to regulate chatbots after Tumbler Ridge is raising privacy fearsappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: To Trump’s tariffs and chaos, world-travelling Carney says ‘not today’
item.description: Prime Minister’s foreign trips rallying new trade allies to our side signals he is preparing for a necessary trade battle with the U.S.

item.title: Amid the AI boom, some VCs see signs of a startup apocalypse
item.description: The postAmid the AI boom, some VCs see signs of a startup apocalypseappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: India has an oil problem. The Iran war makes it worse. Canada is the solution
item.description: Building additional capacity to tidewater secures Canadian jobs, strengthens democratic alliances – and positions Canada to lead as an exporter of solutions

item.title: Hampton Securities CEO Peter Deeb appeals ruling by securities regulator that includes industry suspension
item.description: Panel determined last April he had engaged in trading practices that were ‘a serious violation of CIRO’s rules’

item.title: Canada’s submarine conundrum: Buying a few boats is harder than it looks
item.description: Ottawa will have to perform a delicate balancing act between learning from procurement mistakes, pivoting to new supply chains and meeting North American alliance commitments

item.title: Airlines take a hit from hostilities in the Middle East
item.description: The war will hurt the Gulf carriers—and the whole industry too

item.title: Ontario says it will complete construction on roads to the Ring of Fire by 2031
item.description: Completion of the road network will be five years ahead of schedule, Premier Doug Ford says

item.title: Canadian manufacturing PMI rises to 13-month high in February as new business increased
item.description: S&P Global Canada Manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index, a measure of factory sector health, rose to 51.0 last month from 50.4 in January

item.title: Former Competition Bureau chief Matthew Boswell to join Norton Rose Fulbright
item.description: He oversaw the agency’s unsuccessful bid to stop Rogers’s $20-billion takeover of Shaw, which preceded some changes in competition law

item.title: Trump ponders Plans B to D after his favourite tariffs are taken away
item.description: The replacement duties for those ruled illegal by the Supreme Court may be less vulnerable to legal challengeAlan Beattie
